zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught於1193年出生在愛爾蘭康瑙特時，他的父親William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster33歲，母親Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain20歲。他於1225年4月28日與Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1243年2月17日在法國阿奎泰享年50歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威阿騰利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught于1193年出生在爱尔兰康瑙特时，他的父亲William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster33岁，母亲Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain20岁。他于1225年4月28日与Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1243年2月17日在法国阿奎泰享年50岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 高威阿騰利。

nameMeanings.name: Mór
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ari, Arie, Avi, Eliezer, Erez, Etty, Haim, Hedva, Ilan, Itai, Moshe, Noam.
Jewish (Israeli): shortened and Hebraicized form of some original Ashkenazic surname starting in Mor-.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Mohr   2.

variants.name: Mohr
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone who lived in a fen, Middle Low German mōr.
German, Jewish (Ashkenazic), Danish, and Dutch (of German origin): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle High German mōr, German Mohr, and Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’.
German: from a short form of an old personal name, Morhart (see Morath ).

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught was born in 1193, in Connaught, Ireland, his father, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, was 33 and his mother, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, was 20. He married Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught on 28 April 1225, in County Meath, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 17 February 1243, in Aquitaine, France, at the age of 50, and was buried in Athenry, County Galway, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught est né en 1193, à Connaught, Irlande, son père, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught le 28 avril 1225, à County Meath, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 17 février 1243, à Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Athenry, County Galway, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught 1193 in Connaught, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster 33 und seine Mutter Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain 20. Er hat am 28. April 1225 in County Meath, Irland Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 17. Februar 1243 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Aquitaine, Frankreich und wurde in Athenry, County Galway, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught è nato il nel 1193 nel Connaught, Irlanda, il suo padre, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught il 28 aprile 1225, nel County Meath, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 17 febbraio 1243, nel Aquitaine, Francia, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaughtが1193年にアイルランドコナウトで生まれた時，父William de Burgh Seneschal of Munsterは33歳，母Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briainは20歳であった。1225年4月28日にアイルランドコウントイ メアスでEgidia de Lacy Lady of Connaughtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1243年2月17日にフランスアクイティアインで50歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ガルワイアスエンリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught(이)가 1193년 아일랜드 콘오트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1225년 4월 28일 아일랜드 카운티 미스에서 Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1243년 2월 17일 프랑스 아키테인에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 앳헨리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught nasceu em 1193, em Connaught, Irlanda, seu pai, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught em 28 de abril de 1225, em County Meath, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 17 de fevereiro de 1243, em Aquitaine, França, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught родился в 1193 г. в местности коннот, Ирландия, его отцу по имени William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught 28 апреля 1225 г. в местности коунти мит, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 17 февраля 1243 г. в местности аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности афенри, коунти галвеи, Ирландия.

sm.from 10 March 1226 to 1232: mai le 10 Mati 1226 e o'o I le 1232

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught nació en 1193, en Connaught, Irlanda, su papá, William de Burgh Seneschal of Munster, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Eilinora O’Brien Princess of Thomond, Daughter of Mór Ní Briain, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught el 28 de abril de 1225, en County Meath, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 17 de febrero de 1243, en Aquitaine, Francia, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.
